channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Unsecured sign damaged my car, 12th April
item.description:
I wish to make a formal claim for damage caused to my vehicle by unsecured temporary roadworks signage on Kentish Road, Bexley. On 12th April at approximately 17:52, my vehicle was parked on Kentish Road near 89–91 Kentish Road when a road diversion sign/frame associated with nearby roadworks struck my car and caused scratches and denting to the rear passenger-side door/rear quarter area. My neighbour and his wife, Peter Hattingh of 89 Kentish Road, identified the issue and brought it to my attention. His email is Ptrhattingh@gmail.com. The sign appears to have been left unsecured or inadequately secured. I attach photographs showing the damage, the sign/frame, the restraint arrangement, and the proximity to my vehicle. Please confirm receipt, provide a reference number, confirm whether responsibility lies with Bexley Council or its contractor, and advise where repair estimates should be sent.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 12th April
item.description:
Graffiti

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rochester Way, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole through road surface to loose aggregate pebbles are being removed by traffic going over., 12th April
item.description:
This large pothole near boundary of 48 and 50 Sternhold Avenue has been steadily getting deeper. Now worn through lower layer of tarmac to loose aggregate below, with a risk of flying pebbles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sternhold Avenue, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti outside house, 12th April
item.description:
There is graffiti on the fence outside Home number 48 Lower Rd. This is very upsetting.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lower Road, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.title: Breeze Block left in Cator Park, 12th April
item.description:
A considerate soul feels it’s necessary to leave a breeze block in the park. Please can the council arrange to remove? Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Aldersmead Road, New Beckenham, Greater London

item.title: Someone threw food from their car onto the pavement outside our building. They refused to clean in it up and drove away. Car reg is, 12th April
item.description:
A couple deliberately threw food rest from their vehicle they told me to f*** off (N-word) and drove off. The reg is LW19XFG

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18, Hamlet Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on wall of underpass and wall of walkway heading towards Erith station., 12th April
item.description:
Graffiti has appeared on the walls of the underpass and also the walls of the walkway heading in the direction of Erith station. As this route is used by most people arriving/leaving Erith its doesn't create a great image. Can it be removed please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stonewood Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paint not drying properly from recent painting of the playground, 12th April
item.description:
The seesaw was recently painted the paint has been sat on before drying however it should be dry by now yet it is still wet and my son has now ruined his clothes with paint all over the back of them.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lytham Close, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 220 Edington Road: huge pile of waste at side of recycling bin., 12th April
item.description:
Fly-tipping is at side of communal recycling.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edington Road, Abbey Wood

item.title: Large amount of rubbish by garage and alley way, 12th April
item.description:
Multiple bags of ripped open rubbish Plastic toys and a fence This has been like this for over 6 months and looks dreadful

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Birkbeck Road, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
